matches.snippet: Mons Meg's Monthly, comes out aich Fanagan's Week, to bray at by clownsillies
matches.reasoning: Fanagan's = Finnegan's, directly garbled spelling of the ballad and Finnegan(s) motif; phonetically 'Finn' audible in the name

matches.snippet: On me, your sleeping giant. Estoesto! Estote sunto! From the hold of my capt
matches.reasoning: Speaker claims to be 'your sleeping giant' — directly invokes the HCE-as-Finn motif of the sleeping giant whose body is the Dublin landscape

matches.matched_text: Braid Blackfordrock, the Calton, the Liberton, Craig and Lockhart's

matches.matched_text: my pumpadears in their easancies and my drummers
matches.snippet: Struggling forlongs I have livramentoed, milles on milles of mancipelles. Lo, I have looked upon my pumpadears
matches.reasoning: Drummer epithet; Finn in mythology had druids/wise men; 'drummers' may allude to his retinue or attribute, though pun-oblique

matches.matched_text: drug-tails in my petty-courts and domstered dusty-feets
matches.snippet: I deemed the drugtails in my pettycourts and domstered dustyfeets in my husinclose
matches.reasoning: Drugtails + dustyfeet suggest degradation/fall of a mighty figure; pun-like echo of Finn's fallen state before resurrection

matches.matched_text: two young frish, etoiled, flappant, devoiled
matches.snippet: At the crest, two young frish, etoiled, flappant, devoiled of their habiliments, vested sable
matches.reasoning: Frish = fish, but phonetically suggests Fionn/Finn; heraldic coat-of-arms context fits mythic hero register

matches.snippet: By my natural born freeman's journeymanright and my otherchurch's inher light, in so and such a manner as me it so besitteth, most surely I pretend and reclam to opt for simultaneous. Till daybowbreak and showshadows flee.
matches.reasoning: Daybowbreak = Finn's dawn re-arising; shadowflight = darkness of death/sleep departing; part of the Finnegan wake-resurrection motif

matches.matched_text: I thumbed her with iern of Erin
